title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted instrumented arena and object pool, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in instrumented arena and object pool and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Arena Bump Invariant
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 9249. Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.

steps.instruction:
